old_value: {"id":"tax-4i4","title":"Fix remaining pay periods to use dates instead of upload count","description":"estimateRemainingPayPeriods() and remainingPayPeriods() count uploaded stubs to calculate remaining periods. If a paystub upload is missed, this overestimates remaining periods and underestimates needed additional withholding. Fix: use LatestPayPeriodEnd dates and inferred frequency to calculate elapsed periods.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"brendan.whitney@faraday.io","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:04:50Z","created_by":"Brendan Whitney","updated_at":"2026-03-14T23:04:50Z"}

new_value: Fixed both functions to use date-based elapsed period calculation instead of counting uploaded stubs

old_value: {"id":"tax-9s8","title":"InferPayFrequency returns Monthly for 2 non-consecutive biweekly stubs","description":"With only 2 paystubs from different months, the gap between PayPeriodStart dates is ~28+ days, hitting the Monthly case. Fix: also consider pay period length (end - start) which reveals the actual frequency even when stubs are non-consecutive.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"brendan.whitney@faraday.io","created_at":"2026-03-15T01:22:00Z","created_by":"Brendan Whitney","updated_at":"2026-03-15T01:22:00Z"}

new_value: Fixed InferPayFrequency to use period length as primary signal with gap-based disambiguation

old_value: {"id":"tax-aah","title":"Projection uses sum of uploaded stubs instead of YTD for withheld total","description":"projectForPerson sums individual stub FederalTaxWithheld (CURRENT column) for TotalWithheldToDate. Should use YTDFederalTaxWithheld from the latest stub, like the calculator does. This causes the dashboard projection table to show a lower withheld amount than reality when not all stubs are uploaded.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"brendan.whitney@faraday.io","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:16:32Z","created_by":"Brendan Whitney","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:16:32Z"}

new_value: Fixed projection to use YTD federal tax withheld from latest stub instead of summing individual stub amounts

old_value:
{"id":"tax-5nr","title":"Hourly worker support for EOY projection","description":"## Goal
Support accurate EOY projections for hourly workers whose pay varies by hours worked.

## Background
Salaried workers have consistent paychecks, but hourly workers' withholding varies with hours. A short pay period shouldn't drag down the projection.

## Requirements
1. Extract hours worked from paystubs (for hourly workers)
2. Calculate rolling average of hours to determine "typical" pay period
3. Normalize withholding to typical hours before using for projection
4. Anomaly detection — exclude outlier weeks from rolling average:
   - Unusually short weeks (vacation, sick time)
   - Unusually long weeks (heavy overtime)

## Example
If typical is 80 hours but last paycheck was 60 hours with $800 withheld:
- Normalized withholding = $800 * (80/60) = $1,067
- Use $1,067 for projection, not $800

## Test Criteria
- [ ] Hours worked extracted from paystub
- [ ] Rolling average calculated correctly
- [ ] Anomaly detection excludes outlier weeks
- [ ] Withholding normalized to typical hours for projection","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"brendan.whitney@faraday.io","created_at":"2026-02-07T01:34:07Z","created_by":"Brendan Whitney","updated_at":"2026-02-07T01:34:07Z"}
